meaning: n.(日月周围的)晕轮,光环,荣光vt.使有晕轮,围以光环vi.成晕轮
examples: During winter evenings, Paris's streetlamps have a halo and resemble dandelions.
The second problem is that Venus exhibits a halo of light when it is seen just outside the Sun's disc.
Through a more diffuse "halo effect", whereby its good deeds earn it greater consideration from consumers and others.
Halo 2, out in 2004, turbocharged the popularity of Microsoft s then two-year-old online service.
Now Halo is an international title, but both the developer and publisher are American.
